description_long: This object consists of four irregularly shaped stones arranged in a line, each with a distinct size and form. The tallest stone is upright and columnar, with rough, angular facets and a slightly tapered top. The other three stones are flatter and more rounded, with uneven surfaces and natural edges, suggesting they have been weathered over time. The coloration ranges from dark gray to muted greenish-gray, with subtle mottling and textural variation across the surfaces. The stones appear dense and solid, with a matte, non-reflective finish typical of natural rock. This arrangement could serve as a low barrier or decorative feature in an urban park or along a pathway.
description: A group of four irregular, weathered stones in varying sizes and shapes, featuring rough, matte gray surfaces and natural edges.
description_view_0: Four stones are visible side by side, showing their varied heights and shapes, with the tallest stone on the left.
description_view_1: The stones are seen from the end, revealing their stacked alignment and the vertical profile of the tallest stone.
description_view_2: The stones are viewed from the opposite side, highlighting the flatter stones and the upright stone now on the right.
description_view_3: A top-down perspective shows the irregular outlines and surface textures of the stones, with the tallest stone in the foreground.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or low barrier along a walkway
attribute_pattern: Subtle mottling and natural veining, with irregular, non-repeating patterns typical of weathered stone.
attribute_weathering: Rough and slightly eroded, with visible textural variation indicating exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with landscaped areas, often partially surrounded by grass or soil, and may contrast with paved surfaces.
